title: ISKCON Krishna Balaram Temple, Vrindavan
intro: The ISKCON Krishna Balaram Temple in Vrindavan is one of the most beautiful and spiritually uplifting temples in India. It is a major center for the International Society for Krishna Consciousness (ISKCON).
description:
ISKCON Krishna Balaram Temple – Vrindavan, Uttar Pradesh
Introduction
The ISKCON Krishna Balaram Temple in Vrindavan is one of the most beautiful and spiritually uplifting temples in India. It is a major center for the International Society for Krishna Consciousness (ISKCON). The temple is known for its pure devotion, meticulous cleanliness, and the constant chanting of the Hare Krishna Mahamantra, which creates an incredibly vibrant spiritual aura.
Temple Information
 Temple Name: ISKCON Krishna Balaram Mandir
 Location: Raman Reti, Vrindavan, Mathura District, Uttar Pradesh 281121
 Presiding Deities: Shri Shri Krishna and Balaram
 Founder: A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada
 Significance: A global hub for Krishna Bhakti and spiritual learning.
History and Significance
Established in 1975, this was the first temple built by ISKCON in India. It is built at the site of Raman Reti, where Lord Krishna and Balaram are believed to have played. The temple also houses the Samadhi shrine of Srila Prabhupada, the founder of ISKCON, built in pure white marble.
How to Reach
✈️ By Air:
 Delhi (IGI Airport): The nearest major international airport (approx. 150 km). Taxis and buses are readily available to reach Vrindavan.
🚆 By Train:
 Mathura Junction: The nearest major railway station. From Mathura, you can take an auto-rickshaw or taxi to reach Raman Reti, Vrindavan (approx. 10-12 km).
🛣️ By Road:
 Vrindavan is well-connected via the Yamuna Expressway. Regular bus services and private taxis are available from Delhi, Mathura, and Agra.
Best Time to Visit
 October to March: The weather is cool and pleasant.
 Festivals: Janmashtami and Gaura Purnima are celebrated with great enthusiasm.
Temple Timings
🕒 General Timings: 4:00 AM – 1:00 PM and 4:30 PM – 8:30 PM.
(Note: The temple closes during the afternoon hours. Timings might slightly change based on ritual schedules.)
Nearby Attractions
 Prem Mandir: Located nearby, famous for its architecture and light shows.
 Banke Bihari Temple: One of the most famous temples in Vrindavan.
 Nidhivan: A place associated with Krishna's Raas Leela.
Dress Code
 As it is a spiritual site, modest and traditional attire is recommended to maintain the sanctity of the temple environment.
Facilities for Devotees
 Govinda’s Restaurant: Renowned for serving pure vegetarian Prasad and delicious meals.
 Guest House: ISKCON provides clean and comfortable accommodation for devotees.
 Book Shop: Features a wide collection of spiritual literature by Srila Prabhupada.
 Sanitary Facilities: The premises are exceptionally clean and well-maintained.
Conclusion
The ISKCON Krishna Balaram Temple offers a unique blend of traditional devotion and modern management. The rhythmic chanting and the blissful atmosphere make it an essential stop for any spiritual seeker visiting the holy land of Braj.

faq.q1: To which deity is the ISKCON Krishna Balaram Temple dedicated?
faq.a1: This temple is dedicated to Shri Shri Krishna and Lord Balaram.
faq.q2: Where is the ISKCON Vrindavan temple located?
faq.a2: It is located in Raman Reti, Vrindavan, Mathura district, Uttar Pradesh.
faq.q3: Who built the Krishna Balaram Temple?
faq.a3: This temple was established by A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada.
faq.q4: What is the ISKCON temple famous for?
faq.a4: It is famous for its Harinam Sankirtan (congregational chanting), devotion to Lord Krishna, and a highly disciplined and serene spiritual atmosphere.
faq.q5: What is the best time to visit ISKCON Vrindavan?
faq.a5: The period from October to March is considered ideal for travel due to the pleasant weather.
